quiz.title: SAT English: Unit 5 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: Goal-Oriented Writing — Selecting the Option That Best Accomplishes a Specific Task (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Great Frost' of 1709 was an extraordinarily cold winter in Europe.
  • It caused widespread crop failures and significant loss of life.
  • Elizabeth Blackwell was an 18th-century botanical illustrator.
  • In 1737, she published *A Curious Herbal*, which featured 500 hand-drawn illustrations of medicinal plants.
  • Blackwell created the book to raise money to release her husband from a debtors' prison.

The student wants to introduce Elizabeth Blackwell's work to an audience unfamiliar with it. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Elizabeth Blackwell was an 18th-century botanical illustrator who published 'A Curious Herbal' in 1737.
Created to raise money for her husband's release from prison, 'A Curious Herbal' featured 500 illustrations.
Although the Great Frost of 1709 was devastating, Elizabeth Blackwell's botanical work began years later.
In 1737, Blackwell published a book of medicinal plants after her husband was sent to a debtors' prison.
questions.explanation: Choice A is correct because it effectively introduces who Blackwell was (an 18th-century botanical illustrator) and what her work was (*A Curious Herbal*). Choice B and D focus more on her personal motivations (the prison) rather than introducing the work itself to a general audience. Choice C includes irrelevant information about the 'Great Frost' that does not serve the student's specific goal.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • Methane is a potent greenhouse gas that contributes to climate change.
  • Landfills are a major source of human-related methane emissions.
  • In 2023, researchers at the University of Delta developed a 'bio-cap' made of compost and wood chips.
  • When placed over landfill vents, the bio-cap utilizes bacteria to convert methane into carbon dioxide.
  • Carbon dioxide is significantly less potent as a greenhouse gas than methane.

The student wants to emphasize the function of the 'bio-cap' technology. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Developed by researchers at the University of Delta, the bio-cap is a new technology made of compost.
Landfills are major methane sources, but the bio-cap converts this gas into the less potent carbon dioxide.
The bio-cap utilizes bacteria to convert methane emissions into carbon dioxide, reducing their greenhouse potency.
Methane is a potent greenhouse gas, and carbon dioxide is significantly less harmful to the climate.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it directly explains *how* the bio-cap works (utilizing bacteria to convert methane). Choice A only mentions its origin and composition. Choice B mentions the function but adds unnecessary information about landfills. Choice D describes the gases but completely ignores the 'bio-cap' technology, failing the student's goal.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Tully Monster' (*Tullimonstrum gregarium*) is a soft-bodied fossil found in Illinois.
  • It lived approximately 300 million years ago during the Pennsylvanian period.
  • The fossil features a long snout ending in a claw-like structure and eyes on stalks.
  • For decades, scientists have debated whether it was a vertebrate (like a fish) or an invertebrate (like a snail).
  • A 2023 study using 3D chemical mapping suggests it lacked the skeletal structure of a vertebrate.

The student wants to emphasize the uncertainty surrounding the Tully Monster's classification. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Though discovered in Illinois, the Tully Monster has a snout with a claw and eyes on stalks.
Researchers have long debated if the 300-million-year-old Tully Monster was a vertebrate or an invertebrate.
A 2023 study used 3D chemical mapping to prove that the Tully Monster was likely an invertebrate.
The Tully Monster, *Tullimonstrum gregarium*, was a soft-bodied organism that lived during the Pennsylvanian period.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it explicitly mentions the 'debate' between two classifications, which directly addresses the goal of emphasizing 'uncertainty.' Choice A and D provide physical descriptions or dates without mentioning the classification struggle. Choice C focuses on a potential *resolution* to the debate rather than the uncertainty itself.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The Chankillo Observatory in Peru is the oldest solar observatory in the Americas.
  • Built over 2,300 years ago, it consists of thirteen stone towers arranged along a ridge.
  • The towers align with the rising and setting points of the sun throughout the year.
  • By observing which tower the sun rises behind, ancient people could determine the date within two days.
  • Archaeologist Ivan Ghezzi and astronomer Clive Ruggles confirmed its function in 2007.

The student wants to explain how the Chankillo Observatory was used. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Chankillo Observatory, confirmed as a solar calendar in 2007, is the oldest structure of its kind in the Americas.
Thirteen stone towers were built over 2,300 years ago along a ridge in what is now modern-day Peru.
Archaeologist Ivan Ghezzi used the thirteen towers of Chankillo to prove it was used as a solar observatory.
By observing the sun's position relative to thirteen stone towers, ancient people could determine the date with high precision.
questions.explanation: Choice D is correct because it specifically addresses the *how*—observing the sun's position relative to the towers to determine the date. Choice A focuses on its age and status. Choice B provides a physical description without explaining its use. Choice C focuses on the researchers rather than the mechanics of the observatory.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • Edmonia Lewis was a 19th-century sculptor of African American and Native American (Ojibwe) descent.
  • She gained international fame for her neoclassical marble sculptures.
  • Her 1867 work *Forever Free* depicts two figures celebrating the end of slavery.
  • Her 1876 work *The Death of Cleopatra* was praised for its intense realism and emotional depth.
  • Lewis moved to Rome in 1865 to escape the racial prejudice she faced in the United States.

The student wants to emphasize the diverse themes found in Edmonia Lewis's sculptures. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Working in the neoclassical style, Edmonia Lewis created marble sculptures that earned her international fame in the 19th century.
Lewis moved to Rome in 1865, where she created famous works like *Forever Free* and *The Death of Cleopatra*.
Edmonia Lewis's sculptures range from the social commentary of *Forever Free* to the realistic emotion of *The Death of Cleopatra*.
Though she faced prejudice in the US, Lewis's sculptures *Forever Free* and *The Death of Cleopatra* were highly acclaimed.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it highlights the *themes* (social commentary vs. realistic emotion) of two different works. Choice A focuses on her style and fame. Choice B focuses on her biography/location. Choice D focuses on the contrast between prejudice and acclaim, rather than the themes of the art itself.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • Hyperion is a coast redwood (*Sequoia sempervirens*) in California.
  • At approximately 380 feet tall, it is currently the world's tallest living tree.
  • It was discovered in 2006 by naturalists Chris Atkins and Michael Taylor.
  • The exact location of Hyperion is kept secret to protect the tree from damage by visitors.
  • Centuries-old redwoods like Hyperion can store massive amounts of carbon dioxide, helping mitigate climate change.

The student wants to highlight the environmental importance of trees like Hyperion. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: As a centuries-old coast redwood, Hyperion is the world's tallest tree, reaching a height of 380 feet.
Hyperion, discovered in 2006, is kept in a secret location to protect it from the harmful impact of tourism.
Tall redwoods like Hyperion play a vital role in the environment by storing significant amounts of carbon dioxide.
The world's tallest tree, Hyperion, was discovered in California by naturalists seeking to protect the species.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it explicitly addresses the 'environmental importance' by mentioning carbon dioxide storage. Choice A and D focus on the tree's height and discovery. Choice B focuses on the tree's protection/location, which is a management detail rather than an ecological benefit.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Ghost Orchid' (*Dendrophylax lindenii*) is a rare, leafless orchid found in Florida and Cuba.
  • It lacks chlorophyll and does not perform photosynthesis like most plants.
  • It obtains nutrients through a symbiotic relationship with specific fungi.
  • The orchid is famous for its white flowers that appear to float in mid-air.
  • It is currently endangered due to habitat loss and illegal poaching.

The student wants to emphasize the unique biological trait of the Ghost Orchid. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Ghost Orchid is an endangered plant found in Florida and Cuba that produces floating white flowers.
Unlike most plants, the leafless Ghost Orchid lacks chlorophyll and relies on fungi for its nutrients.
Famous for its appearance, the Ghost Orchid is currently threatened by poaching and the loss of its habitat.
The Ghost Orchid, or *Dendrophylax lindenii*, is a rare species that does not have any leaves.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it specifically highlights the biological differences between the Ghost Orchid and typical plants (lack of chlorophyll and reliance on fungi). Choice A and C focus on its appearance and conservation status. Choice D mentions it is leafless, but Choice B provides a more comprehensive look at the biological 'trait' by explaining the nutritional mechanism.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • Mary Golda Ross was the first known Native American female engineer.
  • She was a great-great-granddaughter of the Cherokee Chief John Ross.
  • In 1942, she joined Lockheed Aircraft Corporation as a mathematician.
  • She was a founding member of Skunk Works, Lockheed's top-secret advanced development program.
  • Her work focused on preliminary design concepts for interplanetary space travel and satellites.

The student wants to highlight Mary Golda Ross's professional achievements. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Mary Golda Ross, a descendant of a Cherokee Chief, joined the Lockheed Aircraft Corporation in 1942.
A founding member of Skunk Works, Ross contributed to design concepts for satellites and space travel.
Ross was a mathematician who moved to Lockheed in 1942 to work on secret government programs.
The first known Native American female engineer, Mary Golda Ross was related to Chief John Ross.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it focuses on her specific professional contributions (founding Skunk Works and designing for space travel). Choice A and D emphasize her ancestry, which is biographical but not a 'professional achievement.' Choice C is accurate but less specific about her actual accomplishments than Choice B.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The Al-Qarawiyyin Library in Fez, Morocco, was founded in 859 CE by Fatima al-Fihri.
  • It is recognized by UNESCO as the oldest continuously operating educational institution in the world.
  • The library underwent a major restoration in 2016 led by architect Aziza Chaouni.
  • It houses over 4,000 rare manuscripts, including a 9th-century Quran written in Kufic script.
  • A state-of-the-art laboratory was added during restoration to preserve these ancient texts.

The student wants to emphasize the historical significance of the Al-Qarawiyyin Library. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Founded in 859 CE, the Al-Qarawiyyin Library is UNESCO-recognized as the world's oldest operating educational institution.
Architect Aziza Chaouni led a 2016 restoration of the library that included the addition of a new laboratory.
The Al-Qarawiyyin Library in Morocco contains a 9th-century Quran and a new state-of-the-art laboratory.
Following its restoration, the Al-Qarawiyyin Library continues to house more than 4,000 rare and ancient manuscripts.
questions.explanation: Choice A is correct because it highlights the library's status as the 'world's oldest,' which is the strongest indicator of historical significance. Choice B and C focus on the modern restoration and laboratory. Choice D mentions the manuscripts, which is significant, but A provides a more definitive statement of its historical importance.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Great Wall of Gorgan' is an ancient defensive system located in modern-day Iran.
  • Built by the Sasanian Empire between the 5th and 6th centuries CE, it is roughly 120 miles long.
  • It is constructed from tens of millions of distinct red bricks, earning it the nickname the 'Red Snake.'
  • The wall featured over 30 forts and was guarded by a garrison of approximately 30,000 soldiers.
  • While less famous than the Great Wall of China, it is one of the most sophisticated frontier walls of the ancient world.

The student wants to emphasize the scale of the Great Wall of Gorgan. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Known as the 'Red Snake,' the Great Wall of Gorgan was built by the Sasanian Empire to defend its frontiers.
Although it is one of the most sophisticated walls ever built, the Great Wall of Gorgan is less famous than the Great Wall of China.
The Great Wall of Gorgan was a defensive system in Iran that housed a garrison of 30,000 Sasanian soldiers.
The 120-mile-long Great Wall of Gorgan was built using tens of millions of bricks and featured over 30 forts.
questions.explanation: Choice D is correct because it uses specific numerical data (120 miles, tens of millions of bricks, 30 forts) to emphasize the physical magnitude or 'scale' of the structure. Choice A focuses on the nickname and origin. Choice B focuses on its fame and sophistication. Choice C focuses on the military population, which relates to size but is less comprehensive than the physical dimensions provided in D.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately select the option that best accomplishes a specific rhetorical goal using provided source material.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether the choice directly addresses the stated goal or includes irrelevant information.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: rhetorical synthesis questions test your ability to match content to purpose. Eliminate options that miss the goal.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: what is the specific task, and which option uses the notes most effectively to complete it?
